title: Stuffed peppers
intro:
Stuffed peppers

Stuffed peppers appear throughout Central and Eastern Europe, the Balkans, the Mediterranean, and the American Midwest. The idea is simple and durable: peppers become cooking vessels for rice, aromatics, and savory filling simmered in tomato broth. Many Balkan versions are braised rather than baked dry, letting the peppers soften while the rice cooks inside and absorbs flavor from tomato liquid. This version keeps that braising tradition while leaning into the EWHF philosophy: rice carries the dish, the sauce builds flavor through technique, and the peppers cook gently in a spicy V8 tomato bath.

ingredientGroups.title: Pepper preparation
items: 4 large bell peppers, tops cut off and seeds removed
1 tbsp olive oil
¼ tsp kosher salt

ingredientGroups.title: Rice filling
items: ½ cup jasmine rice, rinsed and drained
1 tbsp olive oil
1 medium onion, finely diced
3 cloves garlic, minced
1 cup MorningStar Farms Be’f Crumbles
½ cup crushed tomatoes
½ cup stock
2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
1 tsp fresh oregano, chopped
1 tsp paprika
½ tsp kosher salt
½ tsp black pepper

ingredientGroups.title: Paprika–V8 braising bath
items: 2 tbsp olive oil
2 cloves garlic, minced
1 tbsp paprika
3 cups Low Sodium V8 vegetable juice
3–4 jalapeños, sliced
¼ tsp black pepper

ingredientGroups.title: Pepper finishing
items: ¼ cup Low Sodium V8 vegetable juice (for moistening the filling)
8–12 jalapeño slices (2–3 per pepper)

stepGroups.title: Prepare the peppers
items: Lightly salt the inside of each pepper with the kosher salt.
Rub the outside of the peppers with olive oil and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the aromatic base
items: Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook about 5–6 minutes until softened and lightly sweet.
Add the minced garlic and cook about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the MorningStar crumbles and cook 2–3 minutes to warm and lightly brown them.
Remove the skillet from the heat.

stepGroups.title: Build the rice filling
items: In a mixing bowl combine:
the rinsed jasmine rice
the onion, garlic, and crumble mixture
crushed tomatoes
stock
parsley
oregano
paprika
salt
black pepper
Mix thoroughly. The mixture should feel loose and moist so the rice can hydrate while cooking.

stepGroups.title: Fill the peppers
items: Drizzle about 2 tablespoons of V8 into each pepper cavity to help the rice begin hydrating.
Spoon the filling into each pepper, leaving about ½ inch of space at the top so the rice can expand.
Place the stuffed peppers upright in a Dutch oven or deep baking dish.
Place 2–3 jalapeño slices on top of each pepper filling.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the paprika–V8 braising bath.
items: Heat the olive oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook about 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Add the paprika and stir it into the oil for about 10 seconds.
Immediately pour in the V8 while stirring.
Add the sliced jalapeños and black pepper.
Simmer about 1 minute to combine.

stepGroups.title: Braise the peppers
items: Pour the hot V8 mixture around the peppers until they are about halfway submerged.
Cover the pot tightly.
Bake for 65–75 minutes until the rice inside the peppers is tender and the peppers are soft.

stepGroups.title: Finish the dish
items: Remove the lid and bake another 10–15 minutes to lightly brown the tops of the filling.
Carefully remove the peppers to a serving platter.
Place the pot with the remaining braising liquid on the stovetop and simmer several minutes until slightly thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.

stepGroups.title: Serving
items: Spoon the reduced V8–jalapeño sauce onto the plate and place the stuffed peppers on top.
The finished peppers should have tender walls, fluffy rice filling infused with tomato and paprika, and a bright, slightly spicy vegetable sauce.
